narrative.summary: You're on Bloor West in the Annex, ground floor of the new Nomas Hotel, where a café by day pulls a swift transformation into a low-lit bar after dark. Padded booths line one wall, there's a Japanese-Italian menu that doesn't announce itself, and the no-Google-rating-yet energy means the room still belongs to the people who sought it out.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Bloor West in the Annex, ground floor of the new Nomas Hotel, where a café by day pulls a swift transformation into a low-lit bar after dark. Padded booths line one wall, there's a compact bar setup, and the whole room feels like someone's well-appointed front room—intimate enough that a group of four fills it with energy. The light shifts with the hours: bright and easy in the morning, moody and close once the cocktails start flowing.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The Japanese-Italian hybrid plays out in small, precise bites—dashi olives that taste like the ocean with a briny funk, duck tataki sliced thin and seared fast. Mornings lean café: lattes and pastries trucked in from Noctua, the kind of butter-heavy, shatteringly crisp stuff that makes you linger. At night, the menu tightens into snacks and small plates built for drinking, a izakaya-meets-enoteca approach that makes more sense with each round.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Grab a booth in the morning if you want a quiet laptop hang with an actually good flat white, then come back after 9pm with two or three friends when the vibe flips and everyone's squeezed in elbow-to-elbow. It's the kind of spot that rewards being a regular—small enough to feel like you're in on something.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "A quaint space on bloor street, seamlessly blending the cafe and the bar. Great in the morning and even better at night. 10/10 would recommend"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Love the dashi olives and duck tataki! The Café turns into a vibey bar at night. Great intimate space to hangout with a couple friends. 👯‍♀️"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Nice latte, pastries from Noctua. A very chill environment, the booths are comfy. I'll return to try the cocktails!"
